author: y4rr
content:
Result: added three new distributions to ferrotorch-distributions:

**OneHotCategorical** (one_hot_categorical.rs, ~220 lines + 6 tests)
- Like Categorical but samples are one-hot vectors of shape [..., K] instead of integer indices
- Inverse-CDF sampling, normalized internally
- log_prob accepts a one-hot value (or any non-negative weights) and returns sum_k value[k] * log(probs[k])
- entropy: -sum_k p_k log(p_k) (same as Categorical)
- rsample errors out — discrete; refers user to RelaxedOneHotCategorical

**RelaxedBernoulli** (relaxed_bernoulli.rs, ~270 lines + 7 tests)
- Concrete relaxation (Maddison et al. 2017, Jang et al. 2017): continuous distribution on (0, 1)
- Sampling: z = sigmoid((Logistic_noise + logit(p)) / temperature) — reparameterizable
- log_prob uses the closed-form Concrete log density derived from Maddison eqn 21, expressed in α/β form with logsumexp for numerical stability
- entropy errors (no closed form)
- As temperature → 0, samples concentrate near {0, 1}; as temperature → ∞ they concentrate near 0.5
- Tests verify: invalid params, samples in [0,1] (95%+ in interior), low-temperature concentration, log_prob finiteness, log_prob symmetry at probs=0.5

**RelaxedOneHotCategorical** (relaxed_one_hot_categorical.rs, ~330 lines + 8 tests)
- Concrete relaxation of categorical: continuous distribution on the K-simplex
- Sampling: softmax((log(probs) + Gumbel_noise) / temperature) — Gumbel-softmax / reparameterizable
- log_prob uses Maddison eqn 26 (Concrete on the simplex): log((K-1)!) + (K-1)*log(λ) + sum_k(log α_k - (λ+1)*log z_k) - K * logsumexp_k(log α_k - λ * log z_k)
- entropy errors (no closed form)
- Tests verify: shape conventions ([N] -> [N, K] one-hot-on-simplex), simplex constraint (rows sum to 1), low-temperature mode collapse, log_prob finiteness

All three are useful for VAE-style training where you want to backpropagate through discrete-looking samples (Gumbel-softmax for categorical latents, Concrete for binary latents).

Tests: 21 new (6 + 7 + 8). All 305 ferrotorch-distributions lib tests pass (was 284, +21). No new warnings.

Updated lib.rs distributions table.
